KitAdapterDeclaration.description:
Declaration of an adapter binding provided by a Solution Kit.

Supports two declaration formats:

1. **Explicit format** — ``name`` + ``target`` identify the adapter:

   .. code-block:: yaml

      adapters:
        - name: my-adapter
          target: "my_kit.adapters:MyAdapter"
          port: ILLMPort

2. **Implementation shorthand** — ``implementation`` names the class
   directly (resolved at load time):

   .. code-block:: yaml

      adapters:
        - port: IAgentAdapter
          implementation: OpenClawAgentAdapter

A ``@model_validator`` ensures at least one format is provided.

Attributes:
    name: Logical name used to reference this adapter (optional).
    target: Import path in ``module:class`` format (optional).
    port: Port interface name that this adapter implements.
    implementation: Class name resolved at load time (optional).
    description: Optional human-readable description.
KitAdapterDeclaration.title: KitAdapterDeclaration

KitContractDeclaration.description:
Declaration of a contract (schema) provided by a Solution Kit.

Attributes:
    name: Logical name used to reference this contract.
    schema_path: Path to the schema file, relative to the kit root.
    description: Optional human-readable description.
KitContractDeclaration.title: KitContractDeclaration

KitToolDeclaration.description:
Declaration of a tool provided by a Solution Kit.

Maps a logical tool name to a Python callable target using
``module:function`` format — the same pattern as the inline
:class:`~beddel.domain.models.ToolDeclaration`.

Attributes:
    name: Logical name used to reference this tool.
    target: Import path in ``module:function`` format.
    description: Optional human-readable description.
    category: Tool category for taxonomy grouping.
KitToolDeclaration.title: KitToolDeclaration

KitLanguageTarget.description:
Per-language target block in kit.yaml. All four metadata fields below (status, implementation_path, unavailable_reason, dev_note) are optional and additive — kits that omit them remain valid. additionalProperties remain permissive so language-specific fields (package, module, dependencies, tools, adapters) coexist.

status semantics:
  - implemented: source code present in this language under this kit; manifest declarations resolve to real targets.
  - planned: declared but not yet implemented in this language; implementation_path SHOULD point at the future entry.
  - unavailable: cannot be implemented in this language (e.g. framework-specific). unavailable_reason SHOULD point at the peer kit.

Introduced in protocol version 2026-05-09 (Epic K4 unification).

status.title: Status
status.description: Implementation status of this language target.

implementation_path.title: Implementation Path
implementation_path.description: Repo-relative path to the planned/implemented entry point. Recommended when status=planned.

unavailable_reason.title: Unavailable Reason
unavailable_reason.description: Human-readable rationale when status=unavailable. SHOULD reference a peer kit by name.

dev_note.title: Dev Note
dev_note.description: Free-form note for contributors (e.g. library choice rationale, prior art pointers).

KitWorkflowDeclaration.description:
Declaration of a workflow YAML asset provided by a Solution Kit.

Attributes:
    name: Logical name used to reference this workflow.
    path: Path to the workflow YAML file, relative to the kit root.
    description: Optional human-readable description.
KitWorkflowDeclaration.title: KitWorkflowDeclaration

description: JSON Schema for kit.yaml manifest files in the Beddel kit ecosystem

targets.title: Targets
targets.description: Per-language target declarations. Each language target may include status, implementation_path, unavailable_reason, and dev_note fields (see KitLanguageTarget). Additional language-specific fields (package, dependencies, tools, adapters, module) remain permissive.

title: Beddel Kit Manifest
